name: Ash Gourd Juice
description: A cooling early-morning juice made from ash gourd, used across multiple weeks as the first drink of the day.

ingredients.notes: skip if prone to cold; can balance coolness with pepper powder instead
to balance coolness if skipping lemon

instructions: Take a handful of ash gourd pieces.
Add 250ml water and blend into juice.
Filter and drink.
Optionally add lemon or other flavours; can also add ginger.
substitutions: Can add lemon, ginger, or other flavours per preference
tips: Skip if prone to colds, or balance the coolness with pepper powder.
Sample recipe link referenced in source: Instagram reel (Ash Gourd Juice).
storage: Best consumed fresh immediately after preparing.

name: Methi Seeds Water
description: Fenugreek seed-infused water consumed first thing in the morning to support digestion.
instructions: Soak 1 tablespoon methi seeds overnight in 250ml water.
Next morning, strain the seeds and drink the water (some versions: boil briefly, then filter and have warm).
storage: Prepare fresh nightly; do not store leftover strained water.

name: Lemon Water
description: Lukewarm water with lemon, used as an early morning or pre-dinner drink.
instructions: Take 250ml lukewarm water.
Squeeze in half a lemon.
Optionally add 1/2 tbsp cinnamon powder.
Drink warm.

name: Tulsi and Cinnamon Infused Water
description: Overnight-infused tulsi leaf and cinnamon stick water, taken first thing on waking on several weeks.

ingredients.notes: omit if unavailable, use cinnamon alone

instructions: In 250ml water, add 4-5 tulsi leaves and a 1-inch cinnamon stick.
Leave overnight.
Drink the infused water the next morning.
If tulsi is not available, use cinnamon alone.
substitutions: Cinnamon alone if tulsi unavailable

name: Zeera (Cumin) and Fennel Seed Water
description: Warm cumin and fennel seed infused water for early morning, featured in the 'pumpkin week' plan.
instructions: Boil 1 tbsp each of cumin (zeera) and fennel seeds in 250ml water.
Filter and drink warm.

name: Ginger Water
description: Warm ginger-infused water, suggested as an empty-stomach remedy and constipation aid.
instructions: Into 250ml boiling water, add 1 inch of crushed ginger.
Boil for a few minutes.
Filter and drink warm.
tips: Suggested as a home remedy for constipation.

name: Flax Seed Powder Water
description: Empty stomach drink made by mixing flax seed powder into water.
instructions: Mix 1 tablespoon of flax seed powder into a glass of water.
Drink on an empty stomach.

name: Chia Seeds Water
description: Soaked chia seed water consumed 30 minutes before lunch or dinner across nearly all weeks.

ingredients.notes: 1tsp per 250ml in most weeks; 1tbsp per 250ml in BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide

instructions: Soak 1 teaspoon (or 1 tablespoon, per guide) of chia seeds in 250ml water.
Let rest for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Drink 30 minutes before the meal.
tips: Consumed consistently before lunch (and sometimes 15-20 min before dinner) across nearly every week's plan.
storage: Prepare fresh; do not store soaked chia water for long periods.

name: Turmeric Tea
description: A spiced turmeric tea taken after dinner or in the early morning on several weeks; not to be skipped when prescribed.
instructions: Boil 1 glass of water.
Add 2/3 tbsp turmeric powder, 1 small inch ginger, 1 small inch cinnamon stick (or powder), and 1/5 tbsp pepper powder.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Filter and drink.
tips: Explicitly noted: don't skip this when it appears in the plan (post-dinner version).

name: Turmeric Milk (Golden Milk)
description: Warm milk with turmeric, taken 1 hour before bed on several weeks; can use jaggery for sweetness.
instructions: Warm milk gently.
Stir in turmeric powder.
Optionally sweeten with a little jaggery.
Drink 1 hour before bed.

name: Nutmeg Milk
description: Warm milk with a pinch of nutmeg, taken before bed; turmeric milk offered as an alternative.
instructions: Warm 60ml milk.
Add a pinch of nutmeg.
Drink 1 hour before bed.
substitutions: Turmeric milk with ~100ml milk and a pinch of turmeric, in place of nutmeg milk

name: Plain Buttermilk
description: Simple buttermilk used as a pre-dinner drink or evening snack across several weeks.
instructions: Pour 1 glass of buttermilk and drink.

name: Beetroot Juice
description: Fresh beetroot juice taken 30 minutes before breakfast in Week 2.
instructions: Blend beetroot with water into juice.
Drink 30 minutes before breakfast.

name: Apple Carrot Beetroot Pomegranate Juice
description: A mixed fruit-vegetable juice used as a breakfast option in Weeks 4 and 8 (carrot week).
instructions: Juice or blend apple, carrot, beetroot, and pomegranate together.
Optionally mix with coconut water or plain water.
If hungry, can add 8-10 soaked almonds.

name: Apple Beetroot Juice
description: Simple two-ingredient juice paired with poha in Week 6.
instructions: Blend apple and beetroot with water into juice.
Serve alongside 1/2 bowl poha.

name: Spinach Apple Banana Smoothie
description: The program's signature breakfast smoothie, appearing across nearly every week with minor variation (sattu powder, flaxseeds, coconut water/milk).

ingredients.amount: to blend, or use coconut milk/plain water

instructions: Add a handful of spinach, apple, and banana to a blender.
Add sattu powder if using.
Add coconut water, coconut milk, or plain water and blend into a smoothie.
Top with 1 tbsp roasted flax seeds before serving.
Fruits can be altered based on availability.
substitutions: Fruits can be swapped based on availability per source notes
storage: Best consumed within 30 minutes of preparing.

name: Mixed Vegetable Besan Chilla
description: Savory gram-flour pancake (chilla/cheela) with mixed vegetables, used at breakfast or dinner across many weeks.
instructions: Add chopped vegetables of choice to besan flour.
Mix with water into a dosa-like batter.
Pour onto a hot tawa and cook both sides into a chilla/cheela.
Serve with green chutney.
substitutions: Can make with any flour combination as a 'cheela' per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide (e.g., oats, ragi)
tips: No fermentation required.
Reference recipe link: Instagram reel (Ragi Besan Cheela).

name: Carrot Besan Chilla
description: Besan chilla variant with grated carrot, used in Week 6 (Day 1,2) and as part of Week 8's carrot-week lunch/dinner roti.
instructions: Mix besan flour with grated carrots, and optionally onion, tomato, green chillies.
Add enough water to make a dosa-like batter.
Cook on a hot tawa into a cheela/dosa.

name: Beetroot Besan Chilla / Dosa
description: Beetroot-flavoured besan chilla, the breakfast (and sometimes repeated as dinner) dish for Week 9's 'Beetroot Week'.
instructions: Mix besan flour with grated beetroot and water into a batter.
Cook on tawa into a chilla/dosa.
Serve with green chutney.

name: Green Moong Dal Dosa (Pesarattu)
description: Whole green gram dosa, also called pesarattu, soaked and blended with rice. Appears across multiple weeks as breakfast and as a no-oil dinner option.
instructions: Soak 1 cup green gram and 1/4 cup rice together overnight (5 hours minimum).
Blend into a dosa batter the next day.
Add salt to taste.
Cook into dosas without oil, served with chutney.
tips: No fermentation needed - instant dosa.
Reference links in source: Yellow Moong Dosa, Green Gram Dosa Instagram reels.
storage: Batter can be refrigerated up to 1 day; best cooked fresh.

name: Chana Dal Dosa
description: Dosa made from soaked chana dal and rice, served with green chutney in Week 5.
instructions: Soak 3/4 cup chana dal with 2 tbsp rice overnight.
Blend into dosa batter the next day.
Cook into dosas on tawa.
Serve with green chutney.
storage: Best cooked fresh; batter keeps refrigerated up to 1 day.

name: Oats Upma with Green Peas
description: Savory oats upma with green peas, used as breakfast or dinner across multiple weeks.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan, add chopped vegetables of choice (onion, tomato, green peas, etc.) and sauté.
Add salt and lemon to season.
Add oats with a little water and cook until water is absorbed.
Follow the same method as Semiya Upma per the BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide.
substitutions: Can substitute broken wheat dalia for oats using the same method

name: Broken Wheat (Dalia) Upma with Green Peas
description: Savory broken wheat upma, a dinner option in Week 4.
instructions: Heat oil, sauté vegetables of choice with green peas.
Add broken wheat and water, cook until water is absorbed and wheat is tender.
Follow the same general method as Semiya Upma per the BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide.

name: Ragi Malt
description: Finger millet porridge cooked with milk, topped with apple and nuts; appears in Weeks 4 and 5.
instructions: Dilute 2 tbsp ragi flour in a little water to form a slurry.
Add the slurry to boiling milk and cook, stirring, until it thickens.
Let cool slightly, then top with chopped apple, nuts and seeds.
Sweeten with a little jaggery if desired.

name: Kabuli Chana Salad
description: Boiled white chickpea salad with vegetables and lemon, used as a breakfast option in Week 11.

ingredients.notes: cucumber, tomato, onion, capsicum, carrot etc.

instructions: Soak and boil kabuli chana until soft (120g boiled portion).
Mix with chopped vegetables of choice.
Season with salt and lemon juice.
storage: Best eaten fresh; can store boiled chana refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Mixed Vegetable Poha
description: Flattened rice cooked with vegetables; used as breakfast (with green peas) and lunch (with curd) across several weeks.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil and sauté vegetables of choice.
Add salt and lemon.
Add soaked poha and mix well; cook briefly until combined.
tips: Reference link in source: Instagram reel/TV (Poha).

name: Beetroot Semiya Upma
description: Vermicelli upma flavoured with beetroot, used as breakfast in Week 11.
instructions: Heat oil, sauté grated beetroot and vegetables of choice.
Add 1 cup water per cup semiya and bring to boil.
Add semiya and cook until water is absorbed.

name: Grated Bottle Gourd Oats
description: Sweet or savory oats porridge with sautéed bottle gourd, milk and dates; the signature breakfast of Week 7 (Bottle Gourd Week).
instructions: Sauté 1 cup grated bottle gourd in 1/2 tbsp ghee until the raw smell goes.
Add 3 tbsp oats, 80ml milk, sufficient water, and 1 date.
Cook until thickened.
Top with nuts and serve hot.
Can be made sweet or savory.

name: Instant Beetroot Uttapam
description: Beetroot-flavoured uttapam made with besan and oats flour, breakfast in Week 7.
instructions: Mix besan and oats flour with grated beetroot and water into a batter.
Cook on tawa as uttapam (thicker pancake), about 3 pieces.

name: Idly with Sambar
description: Steamed rice cakes served with liquid sambar or vegetable accompaniment, used in Weeks 2 and 7.
instructions: Steam idly batter into idlies (2 pieces).
Serve with a bowl of liquid (thin) sambar or with vegetables, as specified.

name: Ragi and Sooji Instant Uttapam
description: Instant uttapam made with ragi flour and sooji, breakfast in Week 10 (Pumpkin Week).
instructions: Mix ragi flour and sooji with water into a batter; let sooji soften briefly.
Cook on tawa as small uttapams (2 pieces).
Serve with green chutney.

name: Grated Carrot and Oats Cooked with Milk
description: Sweet breakfast porridge of grated carrot and oats cooked in milk and water, topped with nuts; Week 6.
instructions: Cook grated carrot and oats together with milk and water until soft.
Top with nuts and serve hot.

name: Carrot Idly with Liquid Sambar
description: Idly batter mixed with grated carrot, served with thin sambar; Week 8 (Carrot Week).
instructions: Mix grated carrot into idly batter.
Steam into idlies (2 pieces).
Serve with liquid sambar.

name: Fruit and Nut Salad
description: Simple breakfast bowl of mixed fruit and nuts, used in Week 6.
instructions: Chop seasonal fruits of choice.
Mix with a small handful of nuts.
Serve as a bowl.

name: Boiled Rajma Salad
description: Boiled kidney bean salad, breakfast option in Week 4.
instructions: Soak and boil rajma until soft (100g boiled portion).
Mix with chopped vegetables, lemon, and salt to taste.
storage: Best eaten fresh; boiled rajma keeps refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Beetroot Chia Seeds Pudding
description: Chia pudding soaked in coconut milk or beetroot juice, topped with fruits and nuts; breakfast in Week 3.
instructions: Soak 2 tbsp chia seeds in coconut milk, normal milk, or beetroot juice for 1 hour.
Top with nuts and seeds.
Note: do not add fruit if using normal milk (per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide on food combinations).
tips: Avoid combining fruit with milk in the same preparation, per general guideline on food combining.

name: Palak (Spinach) Paratha / Chapathi
description: Spinach-infused flatbread, used as breakfast (with curd) across several weeks.
instructions: Add spinach puree to wheat flour and knead into dough.
Roll and cook as chapathi/paratha (without oil for no-oil version).
Serve with curd.

name: Methi Paratha (No Oil)
description: Fenugreek leaf flatbread served with curd, breakfast in Week 9.
instructions: Knead chopped methi leaves into wheat flour dough.
Roll and cook as paratha without oil.
Serve with curd.

name: Oat Meal with Apple and Carrot
description: Cooked oat porridge with grated apple and carrot, topped with almonds; Week 1 breakfast.
instructions: Grate apple and carrot, cook on low flame for 5 minutes.
Add water and milk in 1:1 ratio and bring to a rolling boil.
Add 3 tbsp oats and cook until thickened.
Top with almonds and other nuts; serve.

name: Boiled Black Beans Salad
description: Simple boiled black bean salad, breakfast in Week 9.
instructions: Soak and boil black beans until soft.
Mix with chopped vegetables and lemon to taste.
storage: Boiled beans keep refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Plain Dosa (No Oil)
description: Simple plain dosa with green chutney, breakfast in Week 7.
instructions: Cook dosa batter on tawa without oil.
Serve 2 dosas with green chutney.

name: Oats and Yellow Moong Dal Chilla
description: Savory chilla made from soaked yellow moong dal and oats, served with green chutney; Week 4 breakfast.
instructions: Soak 1 cup yellow moong dal for 1 hour.
Add 2 tbsp oats before grinding into a smooth batter.
Cook as dosa/chilla on tawa.
Serve with green chutney.
storage: Best cooked fresh; batter keeps refrigerated up to 1 day.

name: Paneer Bhurji with Capsicum (Vegetarian)
description: Vegetarian substitute for the egg omelette breakfast (1 full egg + 2 egg whites with capsicum) used in Week 1, Days 2 & 6. Crumbled paneer scrambled with plenty of capsicum to preserve the original's high-protein, savory breakfast profile.

ingredients.notes: scaled to approximate the protein of 1 egg + 2 egg whites
lots of capsicum, per original recipe

instructions: Heat a small amount of oil in a pan.
Sauté plenty of chopped capsicum until softened.
Add crumbled paneer and cook briefly, stirring like a bhurji (scramble).
Season with salt and any spices of choice; serve hot.
substitutions: Tofu scramble with capsicum (priority 2)
Roasted soya chunks with capsicum (priority 3)
tips: Substituted from the original egg-based recipe per lacto-vegetarian conversion; paneer was chosen as the top-priority substitute and quantity adjusted to approximate the protein content of the original egg dish.

name: Soya Bhurji with Carrot
description: A protein-rich scrambled breakfast using boiled, crumbled soya chunks with grated carrot — a common Indian-household alternative to paneer or egg bhurji.

ingredients.notes: lots of carrot, per original recipe

instructions: Boil soya chunks in water for 8-10 minutes until soft, then drain and squeeze out excess water.
Crumble the soya chunks finely by hand.
Sauté onion, grated carrot, and spices in a little oil; add the crumbled soya and cook 5 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with coriander.
substitutions: Tofu scramble with carrot (priority 2)
Roasted soya chunks with carrot (priority 3)
tips: Soya chunks are a budget-friendly, high-protein, low-fat alternative readily available in most Indian kitchens.

name: Bottle Gourd Chapathi
description: Flatbread made with grated bottle gourd kneaded into the dough; used at lunch across several weeks, often paired with paneer bhurji or curry.
instructions: Grate bottle gourd, add spices of choice.
Add flour (wheat, or half ragi + half wheat, or wheat + besan) and knead into a soft dough.
Roll thin (CD-sized) and cook chapathi on tawa.
substitutions: Flour combination always used 1:1 per measurement guide
tips: Roti should always be thin, about the size of a CD, made with ~30g raw flour or palm-size.

name: Methi (Ragi + Jowar) Roti - Patting Method
description: Flatbread made by the patting method with ragi and jowar flour; used at lunch in Weeks 3 and 6.
instructions: Grate vegetables of choice and add spices and flour; mix well.
Pat the roti by hand on a cloth or butter paper (patting method, used for ragi/jowar since they don't roll easily).
Roast on tawa.
tips: Use patting method for jowar and ragi flours specifically, per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide.

name: Carrot Jowar Roti (Patting Method)
description: Jowar flour roti with grated carrot, made using the patting method; Week 11 lunch.
instructions: Mix grated carrot with jowar flour and spices.
Pat into roti shape by hand (patting method).
Roast on tawa.
substitutions: Carrot chapathi as alternative form

name: Grated Carrot Roti (Wheat + Besan)
description: Roti made with grated carrot mixed into wheat and besan flour dough; the signature lunch/dinner roti of Week 8 (Carrot Week).
instructions: Mix wheat flour and besan with grated carrot.
Knead into dough and roll into roti.
Cook on tawa.

name: Ragi and Wheat Flour Chapathi (1:1)
description: Simple ragi-wheat flour combination chapathi used at lunch in Week 11.
instructions: Mix ragi and wheat flour in equal (1:1) proportion.
Knead into dough and roll into 2 small chapathis.
Cook on tawa.
tips: Flour combinations should always be mixed 1:1.

name: Palak (Spinach) Roti
description: Spinach puree kneaded into chapathi dough, used at lunch for stuffed paneer rolls (franky-style).
instructions: Add spinach paste to the flour and knead into dough.
Make chapathi as usual.

name: Missi Roti (Wheat + Besan + Sattu + Kasuri Methi)
description: Multi-flour roti with sattu powder and dried fenugreek leaves, used at lunch in Weeks 3 and 9.
instructions: Mix wheat flour, besan, sattu powder, and kasuri methi.
Knead into dough and roll into 2 rotis.
Cook on tawa.
substitutions: Bran roti can be used instead per Week 9 instructions

name: Rajma Paratha
description: Stuffed paratha with mashed boiled rajma filling, like aloo paratha; lunch in Week 11.
instructions: Boil rajma until soft and mash it.
Stuff the mashed rajma into roti dough, similar to aloo paratha.
Roll out and cook on tawa as paratha.
substitutions: Kabuli chana can be used the same way (per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide: Rajma/Kabuli chana Paratha)

name: Soya Chunks Curry
description: Home-style curry made with rehydrated soya chunks, used at lunch in Weeks 5 and 11.
instructions: Soak soya chunks in warm water until soft, then squeeze out excess water.
Make curry as you would at home, using minimal oil.
Avoid using any powders like peanut podi or dry coconut podi, per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide.
tips: No premade spice podis/powders should be used in curries.

name: Mixed Vegetable Curry (Home-Style)
description: General home-style vegetable curry, repeated across nearly every week's lunch and dinner with whichever vegetables are available.
instructions: Make any curry as you would at home using available vegetables.
Use minimal oil.
Do not use any spice podis/powders such as peanut podi or dry coconut podi.
substitutions: Can substitute any vegetable for dal or leafy vegetables, per instructions in Weeks 5 and 9

name: Rajma Curry
description: Home-style kidney bean curry, used at lunch in Weeks 2 and 7.
instructions: Soak and boil rajma until soft.
Make into a curry as you would at home, with minimal oil and no premade spice podis.

name: Kabuli Chana Curry
description: Home-style white chickpea curry served with brown rice and curd across multiple weeks.
instructions: Soak and boil kabuli chana.
Make into curry as you would at home, with minimal oil.

name: Black Chana Salad (with Paneer)
description: Boiled black chickpea salad, sometimes mixed with a little paneer and vegetables; used at lunch in Week 6 and as a dinner-style chaat in Week 5.
instructions: Soak black chana for 6-7 hours, then pressure cook for 2 whistles until soft.
For salad: mix boiled chana with chopped vegetables and a little paneer.
For chaat version: temper 1 tbsp oil with onion, tomato, green chillies, add spices (salt, chilli powder, chaat masala), then add boiled chana and cook a couple of minutes.
storage: Boiled chana keeps refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Chickpea Salad with Vegetables
description: Boiled chickpea salad with extra vegetables, lunch option in Weeks 4 and 8.

ingredients.notes: more grated carrot in carrot-week variant

instructions: Boil chickpeas until soft.
Mix with chopped vegetables (extra grated carrot in carrot-week variant).
storage: Boiled chickpeas keep refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Brown Rice with Vegetable Curry
description: Brown rice paired with vegetable curry and curd/salad; one of the most frequently repeated lunch combinations across the program.
instructions: Cook brown rice.
Serve with a bowl of mixed vegetable curry and curd/salad on the side.
substitutions: Rice can be replaced with equal quantity of millets, quinoa, or couscous per measurement guide

name: Bottle Gourd Tomato Brown Rice (Pulav-Style)
description: Brown rice cooked pulav-style with bottle gourd and tomato, served with curd; Week 10 lunch.
instructions: Cook brown rice pulav-style with bottle gourd and tomato.
Serve with curd.

name: Beetroot Paneer Rice
description: Rice mixed with beetroot paneer bhurji, made tasty with spices; Week 9 lunch.
instructions: Make a beetroot paneer bhurji (sauté grated beetroot with crumbled paneer and spices).
Mix into cooked rice.
Adjust spices to taste.

name: Vegetable Biryani with Paneer (More Paneer, Less Rice)
description: Vegetable biryani with extra paneer and reduced rice quantity, served with curd; Week 4 lunch.
instructions: Prepare vegetable biryani with extra paneer and reduced rice quantity.
Serve with curd.

name: Carrot Moong Dal Masala Oats (Pongal-Style)
description: Savory pongal-style oats dish cooked with moong dal and carrot; Week 3 dinner.
instructions: Cook oats and moong dal together with carrot, following a regular pongal-style method (tempered with spices).

name: Green Moong Dal Oats Kitchidi
description: Oats kitchidi made like a regular rice kitchidi but with green moong dal; Week 7 lunch.
instructions: Cook oats and green moong dal together like a regular kitchidi, with tempering and spices.
tips: Reference link in source: Instagram (Dalia Kitchidi/Pongal).

name: Broken Wheat Dalia Kitchidi with Chana Dal
description: Kitchidi made with broken wheat and chana dal; Week 7 dinner.
instructions: Cook broken wheat and chana dal together like a kitchidi, with tempering and spices.

name: Palak Paneer Soup
description: Spinach and paneer soup, lunch option in Week 9.
instructions: Cook spinach into a smooth soup base.
Add paneer cubes and simmer briefly.
Serve hot.
substitutions: Paneer Paratha with salad as an alternative lunch option per source

name: Paneer Stuffed Paratha
description: Wheat paratha stuffed with paneer filling, served with salad and beetroot raita; appears in Week 2 lunch and as an alternative in Week 9.
instructions: Mix crumbled paneer with spices for the filling.
Stuff into roti dough and roll out as a paratha.
Cook on tawa.
Serve with salad and beetroot raita.

name: Capsicum Sabji
description: Simple capsicum-based vegetable side dish, served with roti at lunch in Weeks 5 and 9.
instructions: Sauté chopped capsicum with minimal oil and spices until tender.

name: Leafy Vegetable Curry (Any)
description: Home-style curry made from any leafy green vegetable, frequently paired with roti at lunch.
instructions: Cook any leafy vegetable into a simple curry with minimal oil and spices.
substitutions: Any sabzi/veggie can substitute for leafy vegetables and vice versa per Weeks 5/9 instructions

name: Dal (Lentil Curry, Generic)
description: Generic dal preparation referenced repeatedly across plans, e.g. moong dal soup style.
instructions: Pressure cook 1 cup dal (e.g. moong dal) for 1 whistle.
In a bowl, add 1 tbsp oil, vegetables of choice, the cooked dal, spices, salt and lemon.
Serve hot.

name: Cluster Beans Salad
description: Salad made with cluster beans, lunch option in Week 9 (referenced in source as 'cluster alad').
instructions: Lightly cook or steam cluster beans.
Toss with salt and lemon to make a salad.
tips: Source text reads 'cluster alad' - interpreted as cluster beans salad; flagged as an assumption.

name: Semi-Brown Rice Bowl
description: Semi-brown rice served with chana curry or cluster beans salad; Weeks 5, 9.
instructions: Cook semi-brown rice.
Serve with kabuli chana curry or cluster beans salad, and a side salad.

name: Bhindi (Okra) Curry
description: Home-style okra curry, used at lunch in Week 9 with palak-ragi roti.
instructions: Sauté chopped bhindi with minimal oil and spices until cooked through.

name: Soya Fried Rice
description: Vegetable fried rice with boiled, crumbled soya chunks in place of paneer or egg, for variety and a leaner protein source.

ingredients.notes: lots of vegetables, per original recipe

instructions: Heat oil in a pan, sauté lots of chopped vegetables of choice.
Add soya chunks and stir-fry briefly.
Add cooked rice and toss together with salt and spices.
Serve with salad and beetroot raita.
substitutions: Tofu fried rice (priority 2)
tips: Soak and boil soya chunks ahead of time so they're ready to chop straight into the rice.

name: Paneer Sauté with Mixed Vegetables (Vegetarian)
description: Vegetarian-only version of the Week 2 lunch option '3 boiled egg whites OR 4-5 pieces paneer sauté with mixed vegetables' (Days 6 & 7). The source document already offered paneer as an alternative; this entry standardizes on the all-vegetarian version.
instructions: Sauté mixed vegetables of choice with minimal oil and spices.
Add paneer pieces and toss together briefly.
Serve hot.
substitutions: Roasted tofu sauté (priority 2)
tips: Original document already listed paneer as an alternative to the egg-white option; this entry simply standardizes to the vegetarian path.

name: Paneer Tikka
description: Spiced and grilled paneer cubes with onion and capsicum; one of the most frequently repeated dinner dishes across the program.
instructions: Mix 2 tbsp besan flour with all spices and salt, combine with a little water to form a marinade.
Add onion petals, capsicum cubes, and paneer cubes; mix gently until well coated.
Roast on a hot tawa, or grill in an oven or tandoor.
tips: Same recipe method can be used for chicken tikka, per BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide (non-applicable here as this dataset is fully vegetarian).

name: Sautéed Vegetables with Paneer
description: Simple sautéed seasonal vegetables with a portion of paneer, repeated as a dinner option across multiple weeks.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai, add chopped vegetables (any except potato and raw banana).
Sauté for a few minutes.
Sprinkle salt and spices.
Add paneer pieces and toss together briefly.
substitutions: Tofu or soya chunks can replace paneer
Original BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET guide also lists chicken fry as an option (non-applicable here as this dataset is fully vegetarian)

name: Mixed Vegetable Soup
description: Blended vegetable soup tempered with garlic and chillies, used at dinner across nearly every week, often paired with a protein side.
instructions: Add 500ml water to a pot and bring to a boil; add vegetables of choice and cook until soft.
Let cool, then blend into a smooth puree.
In the same pot, add 1 tbsp oil, garlic, and green chillies; add the blended soup mixture back in.
Add salt and bring to a boil. Serve hot.
tips: Reference link in source: Instagram (Mix Veg Soup).

name: Tomato Soup
description: Simple tomato soup, used at dinner in Week 11.
instructions: Boil tomatoes until soft, then blend into a puree.
Simmer with garlic, salt and spices; serve hot.

name: Pumpkin Soup
description: Pumpkin soup paired with roasted paneer, dinner in Week 10.
instructions: Boil pumpkin until soft, then blend into a smooth soup.
Season with salt and spices.
Serve with 4 pieces of roasted paneer on the side.
tips: Reference link in source for similar soup style: Instagram (Pumpkin Dal Soup).

name: Spinach Soup
description: Spinach soup served with steamed vegetables and grilled paneer; Week 10 dinner.
instructions: Boil spinach briefly, then blend into a smooth soup with garlic, salt and spices.
Serve alongside steamed vegetables and grilled paneer.

name: Steamed Vegetables
description: Simple steamed seasonal vegetables, served as a dinner side, often with paneer or in a soup pairing.
instructions: Steam vegetables of choice until just tender.
Season lightly with salt and pepper if desired.

name: Kabuli Chana Mixed Vegetable Tikki
description: Pan-fried patties made from boiled white chickpeas and vegetables, dinner in Week 2.
instructions: Boil kabuli chana and mash.
Mix in chopped vegetables.
Shape into 4-5 tikkis (patties); if mixture doesn't bind, add 2 tbsp oats flour.
Pan-roast on tawa with a little oil.

name: Beetroot Tikki
description: Pan-fried patties of grated beetroot, carrot, tomato, onion and oats-chickpea flour; Week 5 dinner.
instructions: Grate beetroot, carrot, tomato, onion, and add green chillies.
Mix in 1/2 cup combined oats + chickpea flour with spices.
Shape into tikkis (4-5 pieces).
Tawa-roast with minimal oil.
Serve with green chutney.

name: Boiled Green Peas Chaat
description: Tempered boiled green pea chaat, dinner option in Week 4.
instructions: Soak 30g green peas for 5-6 hours, then boil until mashable.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai, temper with onion, tomato etc., and add required spices.
Add boiled green peas, cook for a couple of minutes, and serve hot.

name: Sautéed Vegetables with Boiled Chickpeas or Rajma
description: Sautéed mixed vegetables with boiled chickpeas or rajma, paired with tomato soup; Week 11 dinner.
instructions: Sauté any vegetables of choice with minimal oil.
Add boiled chickpeas or rajma (40g boiled) and toss together.
Serve alongside tomato soup.

name: Ragi and Chickpea Flour Chilla
description: Savory chilla made from ragi and chickpea (besan) flour with mixed vegetables; Week 5 dinner.
instructions: Mix ragi flour and besan with mixed vegetables and water into a batter.
Cook on tawa as a chilla (2 pieces).
Serve with green chutney.

name: Multigrain Fulka with Bottle Gourd Curry
description: Multigrain flatbread served with bottle gourd curry; Week 7 dinner.
instructions: Mix multigrain flours and knead into dough; roll into 2 fulkas and cook on tawa.
Separately, cook bottle gourd into a simple curry.
Serve together.

name: Oats and Wheat Flour Roti (No Oil)
description: Half oats, half wheat flour roti, used as a lunch/dinner side across multiple weeks.
instructions: Mix oats flour and wheat flour 1:1.
Knead into dough, roll into roti, and cook on tawa without oil.

name: Soya Chunks Curry
description: A light home-style tomato-onion curry made with soya chunks instead of paneer — high protein, lower fat, and a pantry staple in most Indian households.
instructions: Make a light home-style curry base with onion and tomato, minimal oil.
Add soya chunks cubes and simmer briefly until heated through.
Serve with 1 roti (ragi + wheat flour).
substitutions: Tofu curry (priority 2)
Soya chunk curry (priority 3)
tips: Soak soya chunks in warm water 10-15 minutes, then squeeze dry before cooking for the best texture.

name: Soya Keema Franky with Palak Chapathi
description: A spiced soya keema (minced soya granules) rolled into a spinach flatbread wrap — a popular Indian-household protein swap, lighter than paneer.

ingredients.notes: soya granules/mince, boiled and squeezed dry

instructions: Knead spinach puree into wheat flour dough and make a chapathi.
Top the chapathi with soya keema pieces and chopped vegetables.
Roll it up like a franky (wrap).
substitutions: Tofu franky (priority 2)
tips: Soya granules (keema-style) work well here if you can't find chunks — just soak briefly before cooking.

name: Palak Paneer Roti Roll (Franky-Style)
description: Spinach roti topped with paneer and vegetables, rolled franky-style; Week 11 dinner.
instructions: Add spinach paste to dough and make chapathi.
Top with 4-5 pieces of paneer and some vegetables.
Roll it up like a franky.
